calendar.matters:
MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Clifford Chanler (clifford@chanlerllc.com) Plaintiff.
Sarah Ratcliffe Choi (sarah.choi@dentons.com) for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows subject to the understanding that the right to recover fees only once:

PLAINTIFF LAURENCE VINOCUR's MOTION TO TAX COSTS:

The court declines to continue the hearing on plaintiff's motion to tax defendants' cost bills, to November 24, 2020, as requested by plaintiff. The motion is fully briefed, and the hearing will be held remotely, which fully addresses the "COVID-19, air quality and other health-related issues" vaguely cited by plaintiff as a basis for his unilateral notice seeking to continue the hearing.

Plaintiff's motion to tax defendants' cost bills is denied. Defendants are entitled as a matter of right to recover their costs in defending this action, which plaintiff voluntarily dismissed without prejudice after defendants had filed answers to the complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. Secitons 1032(a)(4), (b).) Plaintiff does not meet his burden to show that the challenged costs were not reasonably incurred. (See Jones v. Dumrichob (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1266 ["If items on their face appear to be proper charges, the verified memorandum of costs is prima facie evidence of their propriety, and the burden is on the party seeking to tax costs to show they were not reasonable or necessary."]; see also Ladas v. California State Automobile Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774.)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
